paper.title: Influence of supplementary cementitious materials on engineering properties of high strength concrete
paper.authors: M.A. Megat Johari; J.J. Brooks; Shahid Kabir; Patrice Rivard
paper.material_system: high strength concrete with supplementary cementitious materials
paper.notes: Extracted exact Table 3 compressive-strength values; mixture proportions are represented by cementitious replacement labels and reported w/b because full Table 2 numeric proportions are not in the packet text.

mixture_components.mixture_description: OPC control high strength concrete

conditions.notes: Packet confirms cube compressive-strength results but does not expose full test standard details.

evidence.evidence_text: Inﬂuence of supplementary cementitious materials on engineering properties of high strength concrete M.A. Megat Johari a,⇑, J.J. Brooks b, Shahid Kabir a, Patrice Rivard c a School of Civil Engineering, Universiti Sains Malaysia, Malaysia b School of Civil Engineering, University of Leeds, United Kingdom c University of Sherbrooke, Sherbrooke, Quebec, Canada article info Article history: Received 24 May 2009 Received in revised form 27 November 2010 Accepted 7 December 2010 Available online 7 January 2011 Keywords: Supplementary cementitious materials Workability Strength Elastic modulus Porosity Pore size distribution High strength concrete

Control mix used OPC; other mixes replaced part of cement with SF, MK, FA or GGBS at three replacement levels on a mass-for-mass basis. The control water/cement ratio was 0.28 and the same water/binder ratio was used for other mixes.

main_risks: Full numeric mix-proportion Table 2 was referenced but not present in packet text.
